mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130265.0 thousand as of Jan 2011 indicates ongoing post-recession hiring
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months produce negative 20-day forward returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth, the real interest rate becomes deeply negative, incentivizing equity allocation over cash. This threshold-triggered signal activates only when both conditions hold simultaneously, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as portfolio rebalancing occurs.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.17% and CPIAUCSL at 220.186 as of Feb 2011
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with emerging inflation
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 5-day returns in > 50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 500,000 units annualized signals weakening residential investment demand and forward-looking consumer confidence deterioration. This contraction in a key demand component predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 500,000 units annualized for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 529.0 thousand as of Dec 2010, near threshold
failure_condition: Housing starts below 500k produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered periods, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) remains above 8.5%, indicating a supply-side constraint in a slack labor market. This interaction signals that capacity utilization is stalling despite available labor, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margin compression is anticipated.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.8756 and UNRATE at 9.0% as of Jan 2011
regime_hint: early recovery with supply-side bottlenecks and labor slack
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in > 52% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.8% month-over-month, signaling strengthening consumer demand and forward earnings growth. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors immediately reprice growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 381565.0 million as of Jan 2011
regime_hint: any regime with positive consumer momentum
failure_condition: Months with RSAFS growth > 0.8% produce negative 5-day returns in > 48% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2% annualized while the federal funds rate remains above 1%, indicating monetary policy is restrictive relative to growth fundamentals. This regime-conditional signal activates in late-cycle or slowdown periods, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the Fed is expected to cut rates, but equity valuations compress first due to growth disappointment.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13382.6 billion (Q4 2010) and FEDFUNDS at 0.17% as of Jan 2011
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration and forward earnings risk. This threshold-triggered signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reprice recession probability and corporate profit margins.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.0% as of Jan 2011, elevated post-crisis level
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market fragility
failure_condition: Unemployment spikes > 0.3pp produce positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 3% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously rise above 600,000 units, indicating broad-based demand expansion across residential and overall economic activity. This interaction-driven signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations are revised upward across multiple sectors.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST > 600,000 units for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13382.6 billion and HOUST at 529.0 thousand as of late 2010/early 2011
regime_hint: broad-based economic expansion with housing recovery
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day returns in > 48% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
